title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: Focaccia Genovese: Authentic Rosemary Olive Oil Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil. This traditional Italian recipe brings the taste of Genoa to your kitchen. Perfect for sharing!
longTailKeywords: Focaccia Genovese rosemary olive oil recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Focaccia Genovese with Rosemary & Olive Oil
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil on a wooden board
seo.imageCaption: Golden, airy Focaccia Genovese, fragrant with rosemary and rich olive oil, ready to be enjoyed.

description: Experience the true taste of Genoa with this authentic Focaccia Genovese recipe. This light and airy bread, infused with fragrant rosemary and rich olive oil, boasts a perfectly crisp crust and a soft, chewy interior. It's an ideal accompaniment to any meal or a delightful snack on its own.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the lukewarm water and yeast. Stir gently until dissolved. If using active dry yeast, let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Gradually add 20ml of the olive oil and continue mixing until fully incorporated.

instructions.title: Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Turn the dough out onto a lightly oiled surface. Knead for 10-15 minutes until it becomes smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. Alternatively, use a stand mixer with a dough hook for 8-10 minutes.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Place the dough in a well-oiled bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape and Second Rise
instructions.text: Gently transfer the dough to a well-oiled 30x40 cm baking tray. Using your fingertips, gently stretch the dough to cover the entire tray, being careful not to deflate it too much. Drizzle generously with more olive oil and sprinkle with fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt. Cover loosely and let it rise again for 30-45 minutes.

instructions.title: Create Dimples and Bake
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 220C (425F). Before baking, create the characteristic dimples by pressing your fingers deeply into the dough. Drizzle with a final touch of olive oil in the dimples. Bake for 25-35 minutes, or until golden brown and cooked through. The internal temperature should be around 90-95C (195-205F).

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the focaccia from the oven and transfer it to a wire rack to cool slightly. Drizzle with a little more fresh olive oil if desired before slicing and serving warm.

tips: For a truly authentic taste, use high-quality extra virgin olive oil, preferably from Liguria, both in the dough and for drizzling.
Do not overwork the dough during the second rise or when creating the dimples; be gentle to maintain its airy texture.
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh and warm. If you have leftovers, lightly toast them before serving to revive the crispness.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the sun-drenched Ligurian coast of Italy, where one of the most beloved and iconic breads originates: Focaccia Genovese. This isn't just any focaccia; it's a masterpiece of simplicity, a testament to the power of a few quality ingredients transformed into something extraordinary. With its impossibly light and airy interior, crisp, golden crust, and intoxicating aroma of rosemary and rich olive oil, Focaccia Genovese holds a special place in the hearts of bread lovers worldwide. Whether enjoyed as a breakfast staple, an afternoon snack, or an accompaniment to a hearty meal, this classic Italian flatbread promises a taste of authentic Italian baking right in your own kitchen.

## What is Focaccia Genovese?

Focaccia Genovese, or "fûgassa" in the local Ligurian dialect, is the quintessential focaccia from Genoa, the capital of Italy's Liguria region. Unlike some thicker, breadier focaccia variations found across Italy, the Genovese style is distinctively thin, with a uniquely airy, open crumb and a gloriously crisp exterior. Its signature look comes from the deep dimples pressed into the dough, which serve as little pools for the extra virgin olive oil and brine mixture that moistens the surface during baking, creating pockets of intense flavor and a delightful texture.

Historically, focaccia in Liguria was often baked in wood-fired ovens, sometimes even before the main bread, using residual heat. It was a humble, everyday bread, cherished for its versatility. The key characteristics that define an authentic Focaccia Genovese include a high hydration dough, a generous application of good quality extra virgin olive oil both within the dough and on its surface, and a topping of coarse sea salt and fresh rosemary. The result is a bread that is simultaneously tender and chewy on the inside, with a satisfying crunch on the outside – a perfect harmony of textures and flavors that captivates from the first bite. It's more than just bread; it's a cultural icon, deeply embedded in the Ligurian way of life.

## Ingredients for Focaccia Genovese

Crafting the perfect Focaccia Genovese requires attention to quality ingredients, as their simplicity means each one shines through.

*   **Strong Bread Flour (approximately 500g):** A high-protein flour is essential for developing the gluten structure needed for the focaccia's characteristic chewiness and open crumb.
*   **Warm Water (approximately 400-450ml):** The high hydration is key to the focaccia's airy texture. The water should be lukewarm (around 105-115°F or 40-46°C) to activate the yeast.
*   **Fresh Yeast (15g) or Active Dry Yeast (5-7g):** Fresh yeast provides a more nuanced flavor, but active dry works perfectly well. If using active dry, bloom it in a small amount of the warm water with a pinch of sugar.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil (60ml for dough + generous amount for pan and topping):** This is non-negotiable. Use a high-quality, fruity extra virgin olive oil. It contributes significantly to the flavor, aroma, and texture, making the focaccia rich and tender.
*   **Fine Sea Salt (10g for dough):** Balances the flavors and helps control yeast activity.
*   **Coarse Sea Salt (for topping):** Provides a delightful textural contrast and bursts of salinity on the crust.
*   **Fresh Rosemary (a few sprigs, for topping):** Its aromatic needles infuse the focaccia with a classic Italian fragrance and flavor.
*   **(Optional) Pinch of Sugar:** Helps to kickstart yeast activity, especially with active dry yeast.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Focaccia Genovese

Making Focaccia Genovese is a rewarding process that, while requiring a little patience, is surprisingly straightforward.

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm water with the yeast and optional pinch of sugar. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until frothy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Form the Dough:** Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Pour in 60ml of extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy, sticky dough forms. Do not overmix; a rough consistency is fine at this stage.
3.  **First Rise (Bulk Fermentation):** Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp tea towel and let the dough rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until it has at least doubled in size. During this time, perform a few "stretch and folds" every 30-45 minutes: gently pull a section of the dough up from the edge, stretch it, and fold it over the center. Rotate the bowl and repeat 3-4 times. This helps develop gluten without heavy kneading.
4.  **Prepare the Baking Pan:** Generously grease a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking pan with extra virgin olive oil, ensuring the sides are well-coated. The oil is crucial for a crispy bottom crust.
5.  **Transfer and Shape:** Gently pour the risen dough into the prepared baking pan. It will be very soft and sticky. Drizzle a little more olive oil over the top of the dough. Using your fingertips, gently dimple the entire surface of the dough, pushing all the way down to the bottom of the pan without tearing the dough. This creates the characteristic pockets.
6.  **Second Rise (Proofing):** Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a clean tea towel and let the Focaccia Genovese rise again in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until it looks puffy and airy.
7.  **Preheat Oven and Prepare Topping:** While the dough is proofing for the second time, preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Once the dough has risen, drizzle another generous amount of extra virgin olive oil over the surface. Gently press fresh rosemary sprigs into the dimples and sprinkle liberally with coarse sea salt.
8.  **Bake:** Place the pan in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the Focaccia Genovese is golden brown on top and the bottom is crisp.
9.  **Cool:** Once baked, immediately transfer the focaccia to a wire rack to cool slightly. This prevents the bottom from becoming soggy. Slice and serve warm.

## Tips for Perfect Focaccia Genovese

Achieving that perfect Focaccia Genovese texture and flavor is within reach with a few key considerations:

*   **Quality Olive Oil is Paramount:** This cannot be stressed enough. A good extra virgin olive oil is not just a cooking fat; it's a flavor enhancer that imbues the focaccia with its signature richness and aroma.
*   **Don't Fear Sticky Dough:** High hydration is essential for an airy crumb. The dough will be very sticky, and that's okay! Resist the urge to add more flour during mixing. The stretch-and-fold method helps develop gluten without excessive kneading.
*   **Be Gentle with the Dough:** After the first rise, handle the dough as little as possible to preserve the air bubbles that contribute to its light texture.
*   **Generous Dimpling:** Don't be shy when dimpling the dough. These depressions are vital for holding the olive oil and brine mixture, resulting in those irresistible pockets of flavor and a wonderfully uneven texture.
*   **Hot Oven, Quick Bake:** A high oven temperature ensures a rapid rise and a crisp crust before the interior dries out.
*   **Listen to Your Focaccia:** While baking times are a guide, watch for visual cues. The focaccia should be deeply golden brown on top and sound hollow when tapped on the bottom.

## Serving Suggestions for Focaccia

Focaccia Genovese is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in countless ways:

*   **Simply on its Own:** The most traditional way to enjoy it, perhaps with a strong espresso for breakfast, as is common in Liguria.
*   **With Dips:** Serve alongside a small bowl of high-quality extra virgin olive oil for dipping, or with balsamic glaze, pesto, or tapenade.
*   **Alongside Meals:** It's a perfect accompaniment to soups, salads, pasta dishes, or grilled meats.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice the focaccia horizontally and fill it with cured meats, cheeses, roasted vegetables, or fresh mozzarella and tomatoes for a gourmet sandwich.
*   **Antipasto Platter:** A beautiful addition to any Italian antipasto spread, complementing cheeses, olives, and charcuterie.

## Storage and Reheating

Focaccia is always best enjoyed fresh from the oven, when its crust is at its crispiest and its interior most tender.

*   **Room Temperature:** If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container or wrapped tightly in foil at room temperature for up to 1-2 days. The crust will soften, but the flavor will remain.
*   **Freezing:** For longer storage, slice the focaccia into individual portions. Wrap each slice tightly in plastic wrap, then place them in a freezer-safe bag. It can be frozen for up to one month.
*   **Reheating:** To revive leftover focaccia, avoid the microwave as it can make it chewy. The best method is to reheat slices in a toaster oven or a conventional oven at 350°F (175°C) for 5-10 minutes, or until warmed through and the crust has crisped up again. If reheating from frozen, allow it to thaw first or add a few extra minutes to the reheating time.

Embrace the joy of Italian baking by bringing the authentic taste of Focaccia Genovese into your home. This rosemary and olive oil-infused flatbread is more than just a recipe; it's an experience, a slice of Ligurian sunshine that will delight your senses and transport you to the heart of Italy with every delicious bite. Buon appetito!
